title: Classifier-Free Guidance, Conceptually
concept: Classifier-free guidance instead trains one model both with and without conditioning, then combines their predictions at sampling time to amplify the condition's effect.

exercises.prompt: When you ask an AI to draw 'a cat', how do you think it makes sure the picture actually looks like a cat and not something random?

options.text: It somehow pushes its output to match the idea of 'cat' more strongly
It just guesses randomly every time
It copies a cat photo from the internet exactly

exercises.explain: AI image models have a way to 'lean into' your text prompt so the result matches it more closely. That's exactly what we're learning today!

cards.title: How Does AI Listen to Your Words... More?
cards.body: Imagine you whisper 'cat' to an artist. They draw *something* cat-ish, but kind of vague. Now imagine you shout 'CAT!!' — they draw a much more obviously cat picture. AI image generators can do something similar, turning up how strongly they follow your prompt. This trick is called classifier-free guidance!

cards.title: Classifier-Free Guidance
cards.body: Classifier-free guidance is a technique where ONE AI model is trained to make images two ways: sometimes paying attention to your text prompt (conditioned), and sometimes ignoring it completely (unconditioned). When generating, the model compares both predictions and pushes the result further in the direction the prompt suggests, making the image match your words much more strongly.

cards.title: Two Directions, One Stronger Push
cards.body: Think of a friend giving you directions to a party. First they say where you'd probably wander with NO hints. Then they say where you'd go WITH the hint 'it's near the lake.' Classifier-free guidance takes the difference between 'no hint' and 'with hint,' then pushes you even further past the hint — so you arrive extra confidently near the lake, not just close to it.

cards.title: Prompting 'a red bicycle'
cards.body: The model generates a rough guess ignoring the prompt (could be any object), and another guess considering 'a red bicycle.' Classifier-free guidance measures the gap between these two, then exaggerates it. The final image ends up looking even MORE like a clearly red bicycle than the conditioned guess alone — sharper colors, clearer shape, less ambiguity.

cards.title: Myth: You Need a Separate 'Classifier' Model
cards.body: Older guidance methods used a separate classifier model to judge if an image matched a label, which was extra work and could be unstable. Classifier-free guidance needs NO separate classifier at all — it's just one model trained to work both with and without conditioning, then cleverly combined at sampling time. That's why it's called 'classifier-free'!

cards.title: Old Way vs. Classifier-Free Way
cards.body: Guiding AI generation used to need extra machinery. Now it's built into one model.

left.text: Needs a SEPARATE classifier model to check 'does this look like a cat?' and nudge the image — extra training, extra complexity.

right.text: ONE model learns both conditioned and unconditioned generation, then their difference is amplified at sampling time — simpler and more stable.

exercises.prompt: What does classifier-free guidance combine to strengthen a prompt's effect?

options.text: The model's conditioned prediction and its unconditioned prediction
Two completely different AI models trained separately
A classifier model and a random noise generator
The user's prompt and a dictionary definition

exercises.explain: Classifier-free guidance uses ONE model's two predictions — with and without the prompt — and combines them to push the result further toward the prompt.

exercises.prompt: Classifier-free guidance requires training a separate classifier model to judge whether the image matches the prompt.
exercises.explain: False! That's the whole point — it's 'classifier-free' because no separate classifier is needed. One model is just trained both with and without conditioning.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the idea.
exercises.sentence: Classifier-free guidance trains one model both with and without conditioning, then ___ their predictions at sampling time.
exercises.explain: The model combines (and amplifies the difference between) the conditioned and unconditioned predictions to make the output follow the prompt more strongly.

exercises.prompt: Which scenario best matches how classifier-free guidance works?

options.text: A chef tastes a dish plain, then tastes it with a spice added, and adds extra spice beyond that to make the flavor really pop
A chef hires a food critic to grade every dish before serving it
A chef randomly throws ingredients together without any recipe

exercises.explain: Just like exaggerating the difference between 'plain' and 'with spice' to make flavor pop, classifier-free guidance exaggerates the difference between unconditioned and conditioned predictions to make the prompt's effect pop.

exercises.prompt: An AI image model was trained to sometimes generate images while looking at a text prompt, and sometimes generate images while ignoring the prompt entirely (using a placeholder instead). At generation time, it computes both versions and pushes the final image further in the direction the prompt suggests. What technique is being used?

options.text: A separate classifier network checking image labels
Simple random noise sampling with no prompt influence
Copying training images that match the prompt exactly

exercises.explain: This is classifier-free guidance: one model trained both conditioned and unconditioned, combined at sampling time to amplify how strongly the output matches the prompt — no separate classifier needed.
